objects.name: CyberNetSec.io
objects.description: CyberNetSec.io is a cybersecurity intelligence platform providing timely, accurate, deduplicated, and actionable threat intelligence for security professionals. The platform aggregates, deduplicates, and enriches threat data from diverse public sources, mapping all threats to MITRE ATT&CK and D3FEND frameworks with operationally-focused analysis, detection guidance, and mitigation recommendations. Operated by security professionals, for the security community.

objects.name: Jason Gomes
objects.description: Cybersecurity practitioner and founder of CyberNetSec.io with over 10 years of specialized experience in security operations, threat intelligence, incident response, and security automation. Expertise spans SOAR/XSOAR orchestration, threat intelligence platforms, SIEM/UEBA analytics, and cyber fusion centers.
An AI research and deployment company that partnered with ReliaQuest through its Daybreak Cyber Partner Program to apply frontier AI models to cyber defense.
A cybersecurity firm that joined the OpenAI Daybreak Cyber Partner Program to integrate advanced AI models into its GreyMatter security operations platform.

objects.name: ReliaQuest and OpenAI Partner to Advance AI in Cyber Defense
objects.description:
## Executive Summary
Cybersecurity firm **[ReliaQuest](https://www.reliaquest.com/)** has announced a strategic partnership with **[OpenAI](https://openai.com/)**, joining the OpenAI Daybreak Cyber Partner Program. The collaboration, announced on July 19, 2026, is designed to accelerate the development and application of advanced artificial intelligence in cyber defense. As part of the program, **ReliaQuest** will gain access to **OpenAI**'s frontier AI models and work closely with their technical teams to enhance its GreyMatter security operations platform. The partnership aims to help security teams keep pace with adversaries who are increasingly leveraging AI to automate and scale their own attacks.

---

## Partnership Details
The collaboration is part of **OpenAI**'s Daybreak program, which seeks to partner with industry leaders to apply advanced AI to critical global challenges, including cybersecurity. The key elements of the partnership include:

*   **Access to Frontier Models**: **ReliaQuest** will have access to **OpenAI**'s most advanced AI models and cyber capabilities, allowing them to build next-generation defensive tools.
*   **Technical Collaboration**: The two companies will work together to identify and prioritize high-impact use cases for AI within security operations (SecOps).
*   **Intelligence Sharing**: **ReliaQuest** will provide real-world threat intelligence and insights from its frontline experience, including attacker behaviors and investigation patterns. This data will help train and refine the AI models for practical cybersecurity applications.
*   **Safety and Standards**: As an established AI user, **ReliaQuest** will contribute to developing safety standards and controls to ensure AI is used responsibly and to prevent its abuse.

---

## Impact on Security Operations
The integration of **OpenAI**'s models into the **ReliaQuest** GreyMatter platform is expected to enhance several aspects of security operations:
*   **Accelerated Threat Detection and Response**: AI can help security analysts process vast amounts of data more quickly, identify subtle patterns of malicious activity, and automate response actions, shrinking the time from detection to remediation.
*   **Faster Vulnerability Management**: The partnership aims to leverage AI to help security teams find and patch vulnerabilities more efficiently.
*   **Combating AI-Driven Attacks**: By using advanced AI for defense, organizations can better counter the speed and scale of attacks being launched by adversaries using their own AI tools.

Brian Murphy, founder and CEO of **ReliaQuest**, emphasized the need for collaboration, stating, “Cybersecurity is being reshaped by AI on both sides. The more we can collaborate, the better we can defend against AI-driven attacks.” This partnership represents a proactive step by the cybersecurity industry to harness the power of AI for defensive purposes in the face of an evolving threat landscape.
